data.title: Dogecoin wants to create a "naughty list" for businesses not accepting DOGE payments, aiming to switch them to the "nice list" next year. Merry Dogemas campaign asks users to tag non-compliant companies.
data.description: On Christmas Day, Dogecoin's official account humorously introduced a "naughty list," calling out businesses that have not yet started accepting DOGE payments. In a festive gesture, Dogecoin wished everyone a "Merry Dogemas" and urged the community to identify companies on this list. The account promised to move them to the "nice list" for the next year. The X handle playfully encouraged users to tag businesses that are yet to accept DOGE payments. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Crypto sentiment remains in 'extreme fear' for 14th day as Fear & Greed Index stays low, despite Bitcoin at five times its price during FTX collapse.
data.description: The current Crypto Fear & Greed Index is reflecting lower sentiment levels compared to the time of the FTX collapse in late 2022, even though Bitcoin is now trading at approximately five times the value observed during that period.

data.title: Bitlock Wallet transitions from Telegram to mobile apps; iOS and Android versions in final testing phase for public release.
data.description: In Dubai, UAE on December 25, 2025, Bitlock Wallet, a non-custodial multi-chain wallet that was initially introduced as a native Telegram mini-app, revealed that its iOS and Android apps are now in the last phase of internal testing before public release. The release for both platforms is anticipated to happen soon.

data.title: ABL Bio receives a total of 55 million in R&D funding, with 40 million as an upfront payment and 15 million as equity investment from Lilly, accelerating R&D on Grabody platform.
data.description: ABl Bio, based in Seoul, South Korea, has recently received a substantial 55 million in research and development funding. This funding includes 40 million as an initial payment and an additional 15 million in equity investment. The company plans to utilize this funding to focus on advancing research and development on core technologies, particularly expanding indications for the Grabody platform. This investment reaffirms ABL Bio's commitment to innovation and growth in the biotechnology sector.
